what_it_does: A browser-based design-review portal that lists a hardcoded corpus of mockup HTML files, renders the selected mockup inside a phone or desktop iframe, and lets a reviewer submit structured feedback (mockup filename, optional element, text, timestamp) to a backend endpoint, falling back to localStorage and a copy-paste JSON block if the route is unavailable.
observations: Self-contained single-file HTML tool with inlined gold design tokens and a link to ../design-system.css.
Three-pane layout: gallery rail, device stage, and a feedback / right-hand-man panel.
Hardcoded GROUPS array maps mockup filenames to titles and profile tags (desktop/mobile/tool).
iframe uses sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin" and omits allow-top-navigation.
Desktop mockups render at 1440px logical width and scale down to fit the stage; phone uses 390px.
Backend contract is documented in a comment: POST/GET /api/mockup-feedback with JSONL storage at design/mockups/.feedback/<mockup>.jsonl.
Degraded mode treats any fetch failure (throw or non-2xx) as backend unavailable, stores locally, and shows a copy-paste JSON block.
Preselects SCENARIO-PLAYER.html on boot so the studio opens populated.
Uses CSS custom properties for a warm charcoal/gold theme with Fraunces display and IBM Plex mono/sans fonts.
Feedback statuses include pending, applied, dismissed, and local.
standards_out: The file is a meta-tool for reviewing mockups, not itself a product mockup.
It encodes a backend contract and an explicit degradation path for standalone file:// use.
It hardcodes the entire mockup corpus because file:// cannot fetch sibling JSON.
conventions: Follows the single-file self-contained tool pattern with inline styles and hardcoded data.
Uses data-ui-ref attributes for semantic UI references.
Uses CSS custom properties for a token-driven visual theme.
Breaks typical separation of concerns by embedding all logic, styles, and corpus data in one HTML file.
Uses a relative API path assuming same-origin backend service.
concerns: Hardcoded corpus list must be manually kept in sync with actual mockup files.
No input sanitization beyond escapeHtml when displaying feedback; payload text is sent as-is.
iframe sandbox allow-same-origin preserves the same origin, so loaded mockup scripts could potentially access the parent window.
localStorage fallback can accumulate duplicate entries and never sync automatically with the backend.
GET non-ok responses are silently treated as backend unavailable without surfacing the HTTP status.
Preselected mockup assumes SCENARIO-PLAYER.html exists in the same directory.
Desktop scaling relies on body.clientWidth at render time and may need resize recompute.
notes: A self-contained design-review cockpit for the mockup corpus, letting a reviewer annotate mockups with structured feedback and gracefully degrading to local capture when the backend route is absent.
questions: Is the backend /api/mockup-feedback implemented elsewhere, or is this file intended to be served by a specific dev server?
How are feedback entries promoted from local-only to server-captured status?
Are the data-ui-ref attributes consumed by an external test harness or analytics system?
What determines the 900px desktop viewport height and is it sufficient for all desktop mockups?
Is the hardcoded GROUPS list generated from a manifest, or maintained manually?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a design-review portal that captures structured feedback over mockups before the real build, and the file implements exactly that: gallery, device stage, feedback composer, backend contract, and local fallback.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the design-review loop explicit and reproducible by turning verbal feedback into structured, file-keyed records that an agent or developer can apply to the mockup corpus, while remaining usable as a standalone file.
novelty: It treats the mockup review process itself as a structured data pipeline, with a defined backend contract and a degraded standalone mode that still produces copy-paste-ready JSONL entries.
contribution: A working review interface and capture protocol for the mockup corpus, including a hardcoded gallery index, device-frame rendering, structured feedback submission, local fallback, and a documented API contract that a fused system could wire to a real backend.
summary_for_embedding: STUDIO.html is a single-file browser design-review portal that loads mockup HTML files into phone or desktop frames, lets reviewers submit structured feedback tied to a mockup filename, and falls back to localStorage and copy-paste JSON when the backend feedback route is unavailable.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a self-contained tool for iterating the mockup corpus by capturing structured, actionable design feedback before implementation.
